title: iteadaphne confusa
intro: Iteadaphne confusa, also known as the confused boxwood, is a small, evergreen shrub native to China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Iteadaphne confusa include reducing coughs, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory ailments. Iteadaphne confusa is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or stems in hot water or a solvent.
